title: foxglove.SelectedEntity
description: Information about a selected entity in a visualization panel

source_topic.description: Topic from which the entity originated

source_schema_name.description: Schema name of the source message

id.description: Identifier for the entity. A entity will replace any prior entity on the same topic with the same `id`.

lifetime.title: duration
lifetime.description: Length of time (relative to `timestamp`) after which the entity should be automatically removed. Zero value indicates the entity should remain visible until it is replaced or deleted.

frame_locked.description: Whether the entity should keep its location in the fixed frame (false) or follow the frame specified in `frame_id` as it moves relative to the fixed frame (true)

items.title: foxglove.KeyValuePair
items.description: A key with its associated value

metadata.description: Additional user-provided metadata associated with the entity. Keys must be unique.

pose.title: foxglove.Pose
pose.description: Position of the arrow's tail and orientation of the arrow. Identity orientation means the arrow points in the +x direction.

items.title: foxglove.CubePrimitive
items.description: A primitive representing a cube or rectangular prism

pose.title: foxglove.Pose
pose.description: Position of the center of the cube and orientation of the cube

size.title: foxglove.Vector3
size.description: Size of the cube along each axis

items.title: foxglove.SpherePrimitive
items.description: A primitive representing a sphere or ellipsoid

pose.title: foxglove.Pose
pose.description: Position of the center of the sphere and orientation of the sphere

size.title: foxglove.Vector3
size.description: Size (diameter) of the sphere along each axis

items.title: foxglove.CylinderPrimitive
items.description: A primitive representing a cylinder, elliptic cylinder, or truncated cone

pose.title: foxglove.Pose
pose.description: Position of the center of the cylinder and orientation of the cylinder. The flat face(s) are perpendicular to the z-axis.

size.title: foxglove.Vector3
size.description: Size of the cylinder's bounding box

bottom_scale.description: 0-1, ratio of the diameter of the cylinder's bottom face (min z) to the bottom of the bounding box

top_scale.description: 0-1, ratio of the diameter of the cylinder's top face (max z) to the top of the bounding box

items.title: foxglove.LinePrimitive
items.description: A primitive representing a series of points connected by lines

type.title: foxglove.LineType
type.description: Drawing primitive to use for lines

pose.title: foxglove.Pose
pose.description: Origin of lines relative to reference frame

scale_invariant.description: Indicates whether `thickness` is a fixed size in screen pixels (true), or specified in world coordinates and scales with distance from the camera (false)

items.title: foxglove.Point3
items.description: A point representing a position in 3D space

color.title: foxglove.Color
color.description: Solid color to use for the whole line. Ignored if `colors` is non-empty.

colors.description: Per-point colors (if non-empty, must have the same length as `points`).

indices.description:
Indices into the `points` and `colors` attribute arrays, which can be used to avoid duplicating attribute data.

If omitted or empty, indexing will not be used. This default behavior is equivalent to specifying [0, 1, ..., N-1] for the indices (where N is the number of `points` provided).

items.title: foxglove.TriangleListPrimitive
items.description: A primitive representing a set of triangles or a surface tiled by triangles

pose.title: foxglove.Pose
pose.description: Origin of triangles relative to reference frame

items.title: foxglove.Point3
items.description: A point representing a position in 3D space

points.description: Vertices to use for triangles, interpreted as a list of triples (0-1-2, 3-4-5, ...)

color.title: foxglove.Color
color.description: Solid color to use for the whole shape. Ignored if `colors` is non-empty.

colors.description: Per-vertex colors (if specified, must have the same length as `points`).

indices.description:
Indices into the `points` and `colors` attribute arrays, which can be used to avoid duplicating attribute data.

If omitted or empty, indexing will not be used. This default behavior is equivalent to specifying [0, 1, ..., N-1] for the indices (where N is the number of `points` provided).

items.title: foxglove.TextPrimitive
items.description: A primitive representing a text label

pose.title: foxglove.Pose
pose.description: Position of the center of the text box and orientation of the text. Identity orientation means the text is oriented in the xy-plane and flows from -x to +x.

billboard.description: Whether the text should respect `pose.orientation` (false) or always face the camera (true)

font_size.description: Font size (height of one line of text)

scale_invariant.description: Indicates whether `font_size` is a fixed size in screen pixels (true), or specified in world coordinates and scales with distance from the camera (false)

items.title: foxglove.ModelPrimitive
items.description: A primitive representing a 3D model file loaded from an external URL or embedded data

pose.title: foxglove.Pose
pose.description: Origin of model relative to reference frame

scale.title: foxglove.Vector3
scale.description: Scale factor to apply to the model along each axis

color.title: foxglove.Color
color.description: Solid color to use for the whole model if `override_color` is true.

override_color.description: Whether to use the color specified in `color` instead of any materials embedded in the original model.

url.description: URL pointing to model file. One of `url` or `data` should be non-empty.

media_type.description: [Media type](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Basics_of_HTTP/MIME_types) of embedded model (e.g. `model/gltf-binary`). Required if `data` is provided instead of `url`. Overrides the inferred media type if `url` is provided.

data.description: Embedded model. One of `url` or `data` should be non-empty. If `data` is non-empty, `media_type` must be set to indicate the type of the data.

pose.title: foxglove.Pose
pose.description: The origin of the point cloud relative to the frame of reference

point_stride.description: Number of bytes between points in the `data`

items.title: foxglove.PackedElementField
items.description: A field present within each element in a byte array of packed elements.

offset.description: Byte offset from start of data buffer

type.title: foxglove.NumericType
type.description: Type of data in the field. Integers are stored using little-endian byte order.

fields.description: Fields in `data`. At least 2 coordinate fields from `x`, `y`, and `z` are required for each point's position; `red`, `green`, `blue`, and `alpha` are optional for customizing each point's color.

pose.title: foxglove.Pose
pose.description: Origin of scan relative to frame of reference; points are positioned in the x-y plane relative to this origin; angles are interpreted as counterclockwise rotations around the z axis with 0 rad being in the +x direction

start_angle.description: Bearing of first point, in radians

end_angle.description: Bearing of last point, in radians

ranges.description: Distance of detections from origin; assumed to be at equally-spaced angles between `start_angle` and `end_angle`

pose.title: foxglove.Pose
pose.description: Origin of grid's corner relative to frame of reference; grid is positioned in the x-y plane relative to this origin

cell_size.title: foxglove.Vector2
cell_size.description: Size of single grid cell along x and y axes, relative to `pose`

row_stride.description: Number of bytes between rows in `data`

cell_stride.description: Number of bytes between cells within a row in `data`

items.title: foxglove.PackedElementField
items.description: A field present within each element in a byte array of packed elements.

offset.description: Byte offset from start of data buffer

type.title: foxglove.NumericType
type.description: Type of data in the field. Integers are stored using little-endian byte order.

fields.description:
Fields in `data`. `red`, `green`, `blue`, and `alpha` are optional for customizing the grid's color.
To enable RGB color visualization in the [3D panel](https://docs.foxglove.dev/docs/visualization/panels/3d#rgba-separate-fields-color-mode), include **all four** of these fields in your `fields` array:

- `red` - Red channel value
- `green` - Green channel value
- `blue` - Blue channel value
- `alpha` - Alpha/transparency channel value

**note:** All four fields must be present with these exact names for RGB visualization to work. The order of fields doesn't matter, but the names must match exactly.

Recommended type: `UINT8` (0-255 range) for standard 8-bit color channels.

Example field definitions:

**RGB color only:**

```javascript
fields: [
 { name: "red", offset: 0, type: NumericType.UINT8 },
 { name: "green", offset: 1, type: NumericType.UINT8 },
 { name: "blue", offset: 2, type: NumericType.UINT8 },
 { name: "alpha", offset: 3, type: NumericType.UINT8 },
];
```

**RGB color with elevation (for 3D terrain visualization):**

```javascript
fields: [
 { name: "red", offset: 0, type: NumericType.UINT8 },
 { name: "green", offset: 1, type: NumericType.UINT8 },
 { name: "blue", offset: 2, type: NumericType.UINT8 },
 { name: "alpha", offset: 3, type: NumericType.UINT8 },
 { name: "elevation", offset: 4, type: NumericType.FLOAT32 },
];
```

When these fields are present, the 3D panel will offer additional "Color Mode" options including "RGBA (separate fields)" to visualize the RGB data directly. For elevation visualization, set the "Elevation field" to your elevation layer name.

data.description:
Grid cell data, interpreted using `fields`, in row-major (y-major) order.
For the data element starting at byte offset i, the coordinates of its corner closest to the origin will be:

- y = i / row_stride * cell_size.y
- x = (i % row_stride) / cell_stride * cell_size.x

pose.title: foxglove.Pose
pose.description: Origin of the grid’s lower-front-left corner in the reference frame. The grid’s pose is defined relative to this corner, so an untransformed grid with an identity orientation has this corner at the origin.

cell_size.title: foxglove.Vector3
cell_size.description: Size of single grid cell along x, y, and z axes, relative to `pose`

slice_stride.description: Number of bytes between depth slices in `data`

row_stride.description: Number of bytes between rows in `data`

cell_stride.description: Number of bytes between cells within a row in `data`

items.title: foxglove.PackedElementField
items.description: A field present within each element in a byte array of packed elements.

offset.description: Byte offset from start of data buffer

type.title: foxglove.NumericType
type.description: Type of data in the field. Integers are stored using little-endian byte order.

fields.description: Fields in `data`. `red`, `green`, `blue`, and `alpha` are optional for customizing the grid's color.

data.description:
Grid cell data, interpreted using `fields`, in depth-major, row-major (Z-Y-X) order.
For the data element starting at byte offset i, the coordinates of its corner closest to the origin will be:

- z = i / slice_stride * cell_size.z
- y = (i % slice_stride) / row_stride * cell_size.y
- x = (i % row_stride) / cell_stride * cell_size.x

frame_id.description: Frame of reference for the camera. The origin of the frame is the optical center of the camera. +x points to the right in the image, +y points down, and +z points into the plane of the image.

distortion_model.description:
Name of distortion model

Supported parameters: `plumb_bob` (k1, k2, p1, p2, k3), `rational_polynomial` (k1, k2, p1, p2, k3, k4, k5, k6), and `kannala_brandt` (k1, k2, k3, k4), and `fisheye62` (k0, k1, k2, k3, p0, p1, crit_theta [optional]). `plumb_bob` and `rational_polynomial` models are based on the pinhole model [OpenCV's](https://docs.opencv.org/4.11.0/d9/d0c/group__calib3d.html) [pinhole camera model](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Distortion_%28optics%29#Software_correction). The `kannala_brandt` model matches the [OpenvCV fisheye](https://docs.opencv.org/4.11.0/db/d58/group__calib3d__fisheye.html) model. The `fisheye62` model matches the [Project Aria's Fisheye62 Model](https://facebookresearch.github.io/projectaria_tools/docs/tech_insights/camera_intrinsic_models).

K.description:
Intrinsic camera matrix (3x3 row-major matrix)

A 3x3 row-major matrix for the raw (distorted) image.

Projects 3D points in the camera coordinate frame to 2D pixel coordinates using the focal lengths (fx, fy) and principal point (cx, cy).

```
    [fx  0 cx]
K = [ 0 fy cy]
    [ 0  0  1]
```

**Uncalibrated cameras:** Following ROS conventions for [CameraInfo](https://docs.ros.org/en/noetic/api/sensor_msgs/html/msg/CameraInfo.html), Foxglove also treats K[0] == 0.0 as indicating an uncalibrated camera, and calibration data will be ignored.

R.description:
Rectification matrix (stereo cameras only, 3x3 row-major matrix)

A rotation matrix aligning the camera coordinate system to the ideal stereo image plane so that epipolar lines in both stereo images are parallel.

P.description:
Projection/camera matrix (3x4 row-major matrix)

```
    [fx'  0  cx' Tx]
P = [ 0  fy' cy' Ty]
    [ 0   0   1   0]
```

By convention, this matrix specifies the intrinsic (camera) matrix of the processed (rectified) image. That is, the left 3x3 portion is the normal camera intrinsic matrix for the rectified image.

It projects 3D points in the camera coordinate frame to 2D pixel coordinates using the focal lengths (fx', fy') and principal point (cx', cy') - these may differ from the values in K.

For monocular cameras, Tx = Ty = 0. Normally, monocular cameras will also have R = the identity and P[1:3,1:3] = K.

Foxglove currently does not support displaying stereo images, so Tx and Ty are ignored.

Given a 3D point [X Y Z]', the projection (x, y) of the point onto the rectified image is given by:

```
[u v w]' = P * [X Y Z 1]'
       x = u / w
       y = v / w
```

This holds for both images of a stereo pair.

frame_id.description: Frame of reference for pose position and orientation
Frame of reference for pose position and orientation

items.title: foxglove.Pose
items.description: A position and orientation for an object or reference frame in 3D space

items.title: foxglove.KeyValuePair
items.description: A key with its associated value

metadata.description: Additional user-provided metadata associated with the selected entity.
